info.name: API Testing Framework
info.description:
Postman collection mirroring the Playwright-based API testing framework.

Covers three APIs:
- ReqRes (https://reqres.in) — auth, user CRUD, schema contracts
- RealWorld / Conduit (https://api.realworld.show/api) — auth, articles, comments, profiles
- Mock Server (http://localhost:9090) — payment and notification scenarios

**Recommended run order for RealWorld:**
1. Auth → Register (auto-sets `realworld_token`, `realworld_username`)
2. Articles → Create Article (auto-sets `article_slug`)
3. Comments → Add Comment (auto-sets `comment_id`)
4. Then run Get/Update/Delete/Follow requests freely

**Constraints:**
- Mock Server folder requires `npm run mock` to be running locally
- `{{realworld_token}}` must be populated before hitting authenticated RealWorld endpoints
- Dynamic variables (`{{$randomEmail}}` etc.) generate new data on every run

variable.description: Routes to local mock server — reqres.in is Cloudflare-protected and blocks automated clients

item.name: ReqRes
item.description: Tests against the public ReqRes API (https://reqres.in). No authentication required. All requests use Content-Type: application/json.

exec: pm.test('Response has id and token', function () {
pm.test('User objects have required fields', function () {
pm.test('Created user has correct fields', function () {

item.name: Schema Contracts
item.description: Validates response shapes against the JSON Schema definitions in src/schemas/reqres/. Mirrors schema-contracts.spec.ts. Full AJV validation is not available in Postman — key field presence and type checks are used instead.

exec: pm.test('Schema: top-level fields are correct types', function () {
pm.test('Schema: each user object has required fields', function () {
pm.test('Schema: support object has url and text', function () {
pm.test('Schema: token is a non-empty string', function () {
pm.test('Schema: no unexpected error field', function () {
pm.test('Schema: id is a number', function () {
pm.test('Schema: token is a non-empty string', function () {

item.name: RealWorld (Conduit)
item.description:
Tests against the RealWorld / Conduit API (https://api.realworld.show/api).

Authenticated endpoints require `{{realworld_token}}` to be set. Run 'Register User' or 'Login User' first to populate this variable automatically.

Run order: Auth → Articles → Comments → Profiles.

exec: // Generate unique credentials for this run
pm.test('Response has user object with required fields', function () {
// Auto-capture token and username for downstream requests
// implementations return 409/422. Accept all three as valid.
pm.test('Status is 201, 409, or 422 for duplicate registration', function () {
pm.test('Response has user object with token', function () {
// Auto-capture token and username for downstream requests
pm.test('Status is 401 or 422 for invalid credentials', function () {
pm.test('Response has user with email and token', function () {
pm.test('Status is 401, 403, or 422 without token', function () {
pm.test('Updated user object is returned', function () {

item.name: Articles
item.description: Run 'Create Article' first — it sets {{article_slug}} for all other requests in this folder.

exec: pm.test('Articles array and count are present', function () {
pm.test('Status is 200 or auth error', function () {
// Generate a unique article title to avoid slug collisions
// Slug does NOT change when only description/body is updated — keep article_slug intact
pm.test('Article is marked as favorited', function () {
pm.test('Article is marked as unfavorited', function () {

item.name: Comments
item.description: Requires {{article_slug}} to be set (run 'Create Article' first). 'Add Comment' auto-sets {{comment_id}}. Delete Article runs last to keep the article alive for all comment operations.

exec: // Auto-capture comment id for delete request

item.name: Profiles
item.description: Requires {{realworld_username}} to be set (auto-populated by Register/Login). Follow/Unfollow requires {{realworld_token}}.

item.name: Mock Server (localhost:9090)
item.description:
Tests against the local mock HTTP server (src/mocks/).

⚠️  REQUIRES the mock server to be running: `npm run mock`

Each scenario corresponds to a different registered handler in the test suite. In the Playwright tests, a handler is registered via `mockServer.register(...)` before each test — meaning the same endpoint returns different responses depending on which handler was last registered.

In Postman, start the mock server with the desired handler active, then send the request.

Endpoints:
- POST /api/payments/charge
- POST /api/notifications/send

exec: pm.test('Response has transactionId and completed status', function () {
pm.test('Response has error and GATEWAY_TIMEOUT code', function () {
pm.test('Response has transactionId and pending status', function () {
pm.test('Response time is >= 2500ms (3s delay handler)', function () {
pm.test('Response has messageId and sent status', function () {
pm.test('Response has messageId and queued status', function () {
pm.test('Response time is >= 1500ms (2s delay handler)', function () {
